title: The One With the Ick Factor

lines.text: Scene: Central Perk. Everyone is there.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Tell him.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Tell him, tell him.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Just...please tell him.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Shut up!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Tell me what?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Look at you, you won't even look at him.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (sarcastically) Oh, come on tell me. I could use another reason why women won't look at me.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right, all right, all right. Last night, I had a dream that, uh, you and I, were...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Doing it on this table. (points at the table)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Wow!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Exellent dream score.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Why, why, why would you dream that?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: More importantly, was I any good?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, you were pretty damn good.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Interesting, cause in my dreams, I'm allways surprisingly inadequate. (Monica pats him on his lap)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, last night you seemed to know your way around the table.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I love it, when we share.
Ross goes over to the counter. Chandler follows him.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You're okay there?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I can't belive you two had sex in her dream.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm sorry, it was a one-time-thing. I was very drunk and i was somebody else's subconscious.
Opening Credits
Central Perk, continued from earlier, Chandler is sitting on the table.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hello Rachel.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Get off.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (points at Joey's pen) Uh, uh, gimme. Can you see me operating a drill press?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I don't know. What are you wearing?
Chandler, Monica and Phoebe looks at him

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Pheebs, why would you want to operate a drill press?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Just for some short-term-work. You know, until I get back some of my massage clients.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Pirates again?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, nothing like that. I was just...such a dummie. I taught this "massage-yourself-at-home-workshop." And they are.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, hey, Chan. She could work for you.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (sarcasticly) Thanks Joey, that's a good idea.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What... I could, I could do it. What is it?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, my secretary is gonna be out for a couple of weeks. She is having one of her boobs redused. (Ross looks at her.) It's a whole big boob story.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I could be a secretary.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, you know Phoebs. I don't know if it's your kinda thing, because it involves a lot of being normal. For a large portion of the day.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I could do that.
Ross's beeper goes off

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What are you playing with?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, it's my new beeper.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What the hell does a paleontologist need a beeper for?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Is it like for dinosaur emergencies. 'Help, come quick, they're still extinct.'

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, it's for when Carol goes into labor. She can get me wherever I am. I mean, all she has to do is to dial 55-JIMBO.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: A cool phone number, and a possible name for the kid.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right, I'll see you guys later. (raises)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Off to see young Ethan?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: How young is young Ethan? Young?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: He's... our age.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: When we were?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, he's a senior in college.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: College?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Whoa! And this manchild has no problem with how old you are?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, of course not. It's not even an issue. Cause I told him I was 22.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, I can't pass for 22?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, maybe 25-26.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I am 26.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: There you go.
Chandler's office, he and Phoebe are there when the phone starts ringing.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Can you hear that?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (plays with a thumbtack remover) Yeah?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: See that'll stop when you pick up the phone.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh. Uh, I'm on. (picks up the phone)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (with a deep voice) Mr. Bing's office. (Listens) No I'm sorry, he's in a meeting right now.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm not in a meeting. I'm right... Whoops.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Will he know what this is in reference to? (Listens) And he has your number? (Listens) All right, I'll see that he gets the message. Bye bye.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ross says hi.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ah!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: This is so fun. All right, what do we do now?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, now, I actually have to get to work.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Most likely. (raises and goes toward the door) Okay, I'm gonna be out there.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: All right. Bye bye.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Bye bye.
The intercom buzzes

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (answering it) Yes?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Whatcha doin'?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ooh. (leans against the desk)
Monica and Rachel's, Monica and Rachel are there. Monica is just finishing cleaning the windows.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Windows are clean, candels are lit. Uh, belt's to tight, gotta change the belt. Did I turn the fish? (goes over to the kitchen to check on the dinner) No, cause I made lasagne. (to Rachel) Am I out of control?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Just a touch. Mon, I don't understand. I mean, you've been dating this guy since like, what... his midterms? I mean, why all the sudden are you so... Oh.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Could tonight be the Night?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I don't know. Look he's a great guy and I love being with him but... you know. Things happen, and they happen. You don't plan these things.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So, did you shave your legs?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: A-ha!
Central Perk. Joey and Ross are there.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Would you let it go Ross. It was just a dream. It doesn't mean...
Ross's beeper goes off

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, oh. Oh, oh. Oh this is it. Oh my god it's baby time. Baby time.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right, relax, relax. Just relax, just relax. Be cool, be cool.
Ross dials a number on his cellular phone

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (on phone) Yeah, hi, I was just beeped. (pause) No, Andr?is not here. (to Joey) Third time today. (on phone) Yes, I'm sure... No, sir. I don't perform those kind of services.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Services? (Ross looks at him) Oh, services.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (on phone) Yeah, you want 55-JUMBO. Yeah, that's right. That's right, JUMBO with a U, sir. (pause) No, belive me, you don't want me. Judging by his number, I'd be a huge disappointment. (pause) All rightie, bye bye.
Phoebe and Chandler enter

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, hey. How was the first day?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, exellent. Everyone was so, so nice.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: See, it pays to know the man who wears my shoes. (Joey and Ross wonder what he means) Me.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, I didn't tell anybody that I knew you.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Why not?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, because, you know... they don't like you.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I thought you knew that.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Noho. Who doesn't they like me?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Everyone. Except for uh... no everyone.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What are you talking about?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Don't feel bad. You know they used to like you a lot. But then you got promoted, and, you know, now you're like "Mr. Boss Man". You know, Mr. Bing. Mr. Bing, "Boss Man Bing".
Joey and Ross laughs

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I can't belive it.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, yeah. They even do you.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: They do me?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You know like... uh okay... uh... 'Could that report be any later?'
Joey and Ross laughs

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I don't sound like that.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, oh Chandler...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh... Yeah, you do.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: 'The hills were alive with the sound of music.'
Phoebe, Joey and Ross laughs

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (reaches for hi scones) My scones.

lines.speaker: Phoebe, Joey, and Ross
lines.text: 'My scones.'
Phoebe, Joey and Ross laughs again

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, I don't sound like that. That is so not true.
Joey and Chandler laughs

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That is so not... That is so not... That... Oh, shut up!
Phoebe, Joey and Ross laugh
Monica and Rachel's, Monica and Young Ethan are sitting in the couch.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Did not.

lines.speaker: Young Ethan
lines.text: I am telling you, up until I was, like nine, I thought that gunpoint was an actual place where crimes happen.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: How was that possible?

lines.speaker: Young Ethan
lines.text: Well, think about it. It's always on the news. 'A man is being held up, at gunpoint.' 'Tourists are being terrorised, at gunpoint.' And I just kept thinking: why does people continue to go there? (He checks his watch.) Oh, ah. I should go.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay.
They kiss

lines.speaker: Young Ethan
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Unless...

lines.speaker: Young Ethan
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Uh, ah. Unless you wanna stay over? I mean, I'm going to, so...

lines.speaker: Young Ethan
lines.text: Yeah, I'd really like that.
They kiss

lines.speaker: Young Ethan
lines.text: Uuh, before we get into any staying-over-stuff, there is something you should know.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, is this like 'I have an early class tomorrow' or 'I'm secretly married to a goat?'

lines.speaker: Young Ethan
lines.text: Well it's somewhere in between. You see, in a strictly technical sense, of course, I'm not uh..., well I, I mean I haven't ever uh...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ethan?

lines.speaker: Young Ethan
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Are you a virgin?

lines.speaker: Young Ethan
lines.text: Well, if that's what you kids are calling it these days then, yes I am. I uh, I've kinda been waiting for the right person.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Young Ethan
lines.text: Yeah. You do know I was talking about you, right?
They kiss
[Time lapse. They are now in Monica's bedroom, on the bed.]

lines.speaker: Young Ethan
lines.text: Wow!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You keep saying that.

lines.speaker: Young Ethan
lines.text: You know, you read about it, you see it in the movies. Even when you practice it at home, man oh man, it is nothing like that.
They kiss

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Listen, uh, you told me something that was really difficult for you. And I, I-I figured if you could be honest, then I can to.

lines.speaker: Young Ethan
lines.text: Oh god, don't tell me, I did it wrong.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No-no. Nothing wrong about that.

lines.speaker: Young Ethan
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Um, okay, here it goes. I'm not 22. I'm, I'm 25... and thirteen months.

lines.speaker: Young Ethan
lines.text: Huh!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: But I figured, you know, that shouldn't change anything. I mean, what the hell does it matter how old we are.
They kiss

lines.speaker: Young Ethan
lines.text: Uh, listen um, as long as we're telling stuff, uh, I have another one for you. I'm a little younger than I said.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You're not a senior?

lines.speaker: Young Ethan
lines.text: Oh, I'm a senior... in High School.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ok...ay.
Commercial Break
Monica's Bedroom, continued from earlier.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What we did was wrong. Oh god, I just had sex with somebody that wasn't alive during the Bicentennial.

lines.speaker: Young Ethan
lines.text: I just had sex.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ethan, focus. How could you not tell me?

lines.speaker: Young Ethan
lines.text: Well, you never told me how old you were.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, that's different. My lie didn't make one of us a felon in 48 states. What were you thinking?

lines.speaker: Young Ethan
lines.text: I wasn't thinking. I was too busy fallin'...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Don't say it. (closes Ethan's mouth with her hand)

lines.speaker: Young Ethan
lines.text: ...in love with you.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Young Ethan
lines.text: (nods) Sorry.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well,fall out of it. You know, you shouldn't even be here, it's a school night. Oh god, oh god. I'm like those women that you see with shiny guys named Chad. I'm Joan Collins.

lines.speaker: Young Ethan
lines.text: Who?
Monica and Rachel's, everyone exept Monica is there.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (on phone) Okay, Andr?should be there in like 45 minutes. All rightie, bye bye. (to Phoebe) Just easier that way.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, come on. You told me about the last dream.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, forget it.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, why not. Was I doing anything particularly... saucy?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right, fine. Um, you were not the only one there. (Camera fades to Ross, who's listening very carefully) Joey was there too.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right. (Moves closer.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Was there...uh, huh, huh, huh... andybody, anybody else there.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You're sure? Nobody uh, handed out uh, mints or anything?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, it was just the three of us.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Huh!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So, tell me. Was it like you and Chandler, and then you and me, or you and me and Chandler?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (laughs) You know what?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: There were times when it wasn't even me.
Chandler and Joey laughs, until they look at each other then recoil in horror.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That is so sweet, you guys. (hugs them)
Monica enters, wearing a walkman, so she doesn't hear what the others say

lines.speaker: Ross, Rachel, and Phoebe
lines.text: Hey, Mon.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Mon, Ethan called again. Mon?

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: (shouting) Mon!
Monica takes of her walkman

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ethan called again.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Are you not seeing him anymore?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No. You know, sometimes just things doesn't work out.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And this has nothing to do with the fact that he needs a note to get out of gym.
Monica stares at Rachel

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I, I didn't say any... I sw... I did not say anything, I swear. He stopped by.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Listen, the next time you talk to him, can you ask him which one the strongest Power Ranger is?
Ross and Chandler laughs

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, yeah.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ha,ha, ha, oh my life is just so amusing. Could we drop it now?

lines.speaker: Joey, Chander, and Ross
lines.text: Sorry.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It's morphin time!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Stegosaurus!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Tyrannosaurus!
They all cross they're arms like the Power Rangers do

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ooh, oh, I've gotta go. (raises) Whoa, oh, head rush. One more, and then I have to go. (sits down, and then raises again) Cool!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Where are you going?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Um, oh, I've got a birthday party, with some work people.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Work people? Nobody told me.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, I know. That's a part of the whole, you know, them-not-liking-you-extravaganza.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You know, I don't get this. A month ago, these people were my friends. You know, just because I'm in charge doesn't mean I'm a different person.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, then you should come tonight. You know, just hang out with them. Let them see what a great guy you still are.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You think I should?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I really do, yeah.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, but, could we not go together? I,I don't wanna be the geek that invited the boss.
Chandler's office, he and Phoebe are taking a break from work.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I Think last night was great. You know, the Karaoke thing. Tracy and I doing Ebony and Ivory.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You were great. But they still made fun of you.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You know, now you're more like, you know like, "Mr. Caring Boss," "Mr.", you know, "I'm one of you, Boss," "Mr., I wanna be your buddy, Boss Man Bing!"

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Then, I don't get it.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, you know what Chandler? I think you've gotta face it. You're like, the guy in the big office, you know. You're the one that hires them, that fires them... They still say you're a great boss.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: They do?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Uh huh. But they're not your friends anymore.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I just wan't to...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, but you can't.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: But I just wa...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Uh uh.
Central Perk. Everyone exept Phoebe and Chandler is there. Ross's beeper goes off and everyone exept him react.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Aren't you gonna...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, Carol and I have a new system. If she punches in 911, it means she's having a baby, otherwise I just ignore it.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What about Andr?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, well this morning he got a call from who I think was our cousin Nathan, and frankly, it was a little more than I needed to know.
Ethan enters

lines.speaker: Young Ethan
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That was gonna be my opener.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (understands that Monica and Ethan wanna be alone) Hey, did you guys check out those new hand-dryers in the bathroom?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I thought that was just a rumour.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: True story.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: They're here already?
Rachel, Ross and Ross go to the bathroom

lines.speaker: Young Ethan
lines.text: All right, look. I've gotta tell you something. I'm not 17. I only said so that you'd think I was cute and vunerable. I'm actually 30, I have a wife, I have a job, I'm your Congressman. Monica, this is ridiculous, we're great together. We can talk, we make each other laugh, and the sex. Oh, man, okay i have no frame of graft, but I thought that was great.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It was.

lines.speaker: Young Ethan
lines.text: Then, what's the problem?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ethan, it's um... it's icky.

lines.speaker: Young Ethan
lines.text: Icky? You're actually gonna throw this away because it's icky?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: This isn't easy for me either. I wish things were different, I... If you were a few years older, or if I was a few years younger, or if we lived in biblical times, I would really...

lines.speaker: Young Ethan
lines.text: No, don't say it. (closes Monica's mouth with his hand)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: ...love you.
Ross, Rachel and Joey come back from the bathroom. They discover that Monica and Ethan aren't finished talking to each other yet.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Are you're hands still wet?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh, moist, yeah.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Let's dry 'em again.
They go to the bathroom again
A hall on the floor where Chandler works. Chandler and Phoebe enters, and overhears some employees's conversation. One of them is doing Chandler.

lines.speaker: Gerston
lines.text: Uh, like, could these margaritas be any stronger? (They discover that Chandler is listening) Hey, Chandler.

lines.speaker: Santos
lines.text: Hello, Mr. Bing.

lines.speaker: Petrie
lines.text: Loved your Stevie Wonder last night.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Thanks. Listen, about the weekly numbers, I'm gonna need them on my desk by nine o'clock.

lines.speaker: Santos
lines.text: Sure.

lines.speaker: Gerston
lines.text: No problem.
They go away, trying very hard not to laugh at Chandler

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You have to give 'em something, you know. Okay, now that was Gerston, Santos, and who's the guy with the moustache?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Petrie.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Petrie, right, right. Okay, some people gonna be working this weekend.
Monica and Rachel's, Ross is watching TV, but turns it off, and Rachel is sleeping on the couch. Ross puts a blanket over her.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (talking in her sleep) Oooooooooh. (Rachel strokes her hand over the pillow. Ross mimicks her silently) Oh, that's nice. Oh, oh. Huh, Ross!
Ross gets all excited and starts to dance on the coffee table, but slips allmost immediatly, and falls onto the couch. Rachel wakes up.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm here.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You are. Well, um... We, we, we were just...  Wow!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What? (his beeper goes off) Great, now I'm having a baby.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ooh, Ooh.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm having... I'm having a baby. (jumps back onto the table again) I'm having a... Where's the phone? The phone?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don't know where the phone is.
Ross runs from the table, over the couch but slips and falls onto the floor

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm hurt.
Closing Credits
The Hallway, Ross is eagerly waiting for the others to get ready, to go to the hospital.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Monica, let's go. Come on now people, woman in labor.
Chandler struts out from his apartment

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (doing a little dance) Hey Ross, look what I've got going here.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, save it for the cab, okay.
Rachel comes out from their apartment with a mirror and a lipstick in her hands

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What are you doing? We're going to a hospital.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What, so I can't lokk nice? There might be doctors there.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Joey, get out of the fridge.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right, all right. (he comes out from their apartment with a huge sandwich in his hand)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What is that? (refering to the sandwich)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: For the ride.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, like in a cab...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Save it.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, hating this.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Monica, come on now. Let's go, baby coming.
Monica enters from their apartment, crying

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I can't belive it, I'm gonna be an aunt. I'm gonna have like a nephew.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That's nice. Get out Let's go, come on.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right, I'm going. I'm going.
They all go down the stairs, but Ross turns around, looking like his in a coma. The others also turns around to get him.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Here we go, here we go.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Rossy, Rossy.
End
